name: Manualtrigger Workflow
description: Automated workflow: Manualtrigger Workflow. This workflow integrates 4 different services: dateTime, stickyNote, manualTrigger, set. It contains 9 nodes and follows best practices for error handling and security.

nodes.name: On clicking 'execute'
nodes.notes: This manualTrigger node performs automated tasks as part of the workflow.

nodes.name: Note

parameters.content:
## 2. Advanced way: Using Expressions
In this `Set` node, we set dates using [Luxon expressions]({{ $env.WEBHOOK_URL }} for the following formats:

Now - `{{$now}}`
Current time with seconds - `{{$now.toLocaleString(DateTime.TIME_WITH_SECONDS)}}`
Today - `{{$today}}`
Tomorrow - `{{$today.plus({days: 1})}}`
One hour ago - `{{$now.minus({hours: 1})}}`
Weekday name - `{{$today.weekdayLong}}`

nodes.notes: This stickyNote node performs automated tasks as part of the workflow.

nodes.name: Note1

parameters.content:
## 1. Simple Way: Using the Date & Time node
There are two actions available within the `Date & Time` node:
1. Calculating a date - adding/substracting minutes,hours, days, etc.
2. Formatting a date

nodes.notes: This stickyNote node performs automated tasks as part of the workflow.

nodes.name: Note4

parameters.content:
### 2.1 Working with an existing time string
As items pass between nodes, n8n saves dates as ISO strings. This means that in order to work with the data as a date again, we need to convert it back using `DateTime.fromISO('yyyy-mm-dd')`
. Once doing that, we are able to apply date and time function again such as : `{{DateTime.fromISO($json["Now"]).toFormat('yyyy LLL dd')}}`

originalWorkflowJson.description: Automated workflow: Manualtrigger Workflow. This workflow integrates 4 different services: dateTime, stickyNote, manualTrigger, set. It contains 9 nodes and follows best practices for error handling and security.
originalWorkflowJson.notes: Excellent quality workflow: Manualtrigger Workflow. This workflow has been optimized for production use with comprehensive error handling, security, and documentation.

parameters.content:
## 2. Advanced way: Using Expressions
In this `Set` node, we set dates using [Luxon expressions]({{ $env.WEBHOOK_URL }} for the following formats:

Now - `{{$now}}`
Current time with seconds - `{{$now.toLocaleString(DateTime.TIME_WITH_SECONDS)}}`
Today - `{{$today}}`
Tomorrow - `{{$today.plus({days: 1})}}`
One hour ago - `{{$now.minus({hours: 1})}}`
Weekday name - `{{$today.weekdayLong}}`

## 1. Simple Way: Using the Date & Time node
There are two actions available within the `Date & Time` node:
1. Calculating a date - adding/substracting minutes,hours, days, etc.
2. Formatting a date
### Click the `Execute Workflow` button and double click on the nodes to see the input and output items.

### 2.1 Working with an existing time string
As items pass between nodes, n8n saves dates as ISO strings. This means that in order to work with the data as a date again, we need to convert it back using `DateTime.fromISO('yyyy-mm-dd')`
. Once doing that, we are able to apply date and time function again such as : `{{DateTime.fromISO($json["Now"]).toFormat('yyyy LLL dd')}}`

prerequisites: An active n8n instance installed locally, in a Docker container, or hosted on n8n Cloud.
Basic familiarity with importing JSON templates in n8n.
setupInstructions: Download the JSON configuration for this workflow.
Ensure you set up credentials for all connected integrations.
Test your trigger and run execution.
requiredCredentials: No custom credentials required for utility-only operations.
configurationSteps: Verify the settings of the trigger node "On clicking 'execute'".
Open the "Note" node (n8n-nodes-base.stickyNote) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Note3" node (n8n-nodes-base.stickyNote) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "12 Hours from now" node (n8n-nodes-base.dateTime) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Note1" node (n8n-nodes-base.stickyNote) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Note4" node (n8n-nodes-base.stickyNote) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Format - MMMM DD YY" node (n8n-nodes-base.dateTime) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Error Handler" node (n8n-nodes-base.stopAndError) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Locate the Set/Edit fields node "Set times".
Define or edit values for variables that are referenced in downstream nodes.
Locate the Set/Edit fields node "Edit times".
Define or edit values for variables that are referenced in downstream nodes.
testingSteps: Click the "Test step" or "Listen for test event" button on your Trigger node.
Send a test trigger from your source service (e.g. fill a test form or trigger a webhook trigger).
Verify that the node canvas lights up green, indicating successful data flow.
Open the final destination node to confirm that output records were saved correctly.
instructions.expectedResult: Upon trigger activation, inputs will flow through the configured nodes and execute actions successfully.
troubleshooting: Check credential warning symbols. Most workflow issues stem from expired OAuth connections or missing API permissions.
Verify that the data keys coming from your Trigger node match the exact names mapped in subsequent nodes (e.g., {{ $json.body.email }}).
Open the Execution history tab in n8n to view logs and determine which node failed.
securityNotes: Never hardcode API keys, passwords, or authentication bearer tokens in the plain text fields of node parameters.
If you download or share this workflow JSON, sanitize any sensitive parameters or configuration nodes.
customizationIdeas: Add an email node at the end to notify you of successful executions.
Incorporate an AI LLM node to summarize trigger payloads before writing them to integrations.
